pi_items.source: Checked setup_task.sh SQL query and gnuhealth-50-demo.sql dump for party 'John Zenon'.
Checked setup_task.sh SQL query and gnuhealth-50-demo.sql dump for party 'John Zenon'.
Checked verifier.py logic which explicitly verifies if the active diagnosis code starts with 'T59'.
Checked verifier.py logic which requires heart rate (hr) to be >= 100 for full score.
Checked verifier.py logic which requires oxygen saturation (osat) to be <= 95 for full score.
Checked verifier.py logic which checks if new_lab_count >= 2 for the full 20 points.
Checked verifier.py logic which checks if new_prescription_count >= 1.
Checked verifier.py logic which expects the follow-up appointment difference (days_diff) to be >= 1.
Checked verifier.py logic which expects the follow-up appointment difference (days_diff) to be <= 7.
Matches the explicit role assigned to the user in the task description.
Matches the contextual setting of the task involving a forklift operator in a warehouse.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated demo data within the GNU Health database. The target patient is correctly identified as John Zenon. To pass the verifier, the agent must document a clinical evaluation with a heart rate of at least 100 and O2 saturation of at most 95. They must add an active diagnosis starting with the ICD-10 prefix 'T59'. Additionally, the agent needs to order at least 2 new lab tests, prescribe at least 1 medication, and schedule a follow-up appointment between 1 and 7 days from the task start date.
